content.problemStatement: You are given an integer sequence AAA with length NNN. In one move, you are allowed to choose one element of this sequence and change it to an arbitrary integer. Compute the minimum number of moves needed to make the sequence a permutation of the integers 111 through NNN.
constraints: The sum of N for all test cases does not exceed 2*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum required number of moves.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, we can change the last element to 4 to get the permutation [1, 2, 3, 4]. For the second test case, we can change the first element to 1 and the second element to 3 to get the permutation [1, 3, 2].

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy approach. We first count the frequency of each number in the array. Then, we iterate over the numbers from 1 to N and check if each number is present in the array. If a number is not present, we increment the count of moves. If a number is present more than once, we also increment the count of moves. The minimum required number of moves is the count of moves.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum size of the array. This is because we are iterating over the array for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the maximum size of the array. This is because we are using a frequency array to store the frequency of each number.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    arr.sort()
    moves = 0
    j = 1
    for i in range(N):
        if arr[i] != j:
            moves += 1
        else:
            j += 1
    print(moves)
